lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me a quest!

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Give me a quest what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me a quest please.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Well seeing as you asked nicely... I could do with some help.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: The wizard Grayzag next door decided he didn't like me so he enlisted an army of hundreds of imps.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: These imps stole all sorts of my things. Most of these things I don't really care about, just eggs and balls of string and things.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: But they stole my four magical beads. There was a red one, a yellow one, a black one, and a white one.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: These imps have now spread out all over the kingdom. Could you get my beads back for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well this is a surprising turn of events!

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I just so happen to have all of those beads on me!

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Are you saying that you stole my beads all this time and I’ve been blaming these imps!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not at all! I just found them throughout my travels and presumed somebody would need them at some point.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Bah! Fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll try.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: That's great, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've better things to do than chase imps.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Well if you're not interested in the quests I have to give you, don't waste my time by asking me for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me a quest or else!

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Or else what? You'll attack me?

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Hahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just stop messing around and give me a quest!

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Ah now you're assuming I have one to give.

sections.title: Speaking to Wizard Mizgog with no beads

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: So how are you doing finding my beads?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've not found any yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Well get on with it. I've lost a white bead, a red bead, a black bead, and a yellow bead. Go kill some imps!

sections.title: Speaking to Wizard Mizgog with some beads

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: So how are you doing finding my beads?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have found some of your beads.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Come back when you have them all. The colour of the four beads that I need are red, yellow, black, and white. Go chase some imps!

lines.speaker: Wizard Grayzag
lines.text: You're a fool, [player name]. Do you really think you'll find four imps out of thousands? Good luck. Ha!

sections.title: Giving the beads to Wizard Mizgog

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: So how are you doing finding my beads?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got all four beads. It was hard work I can tell you.

lines.speaker: Wizard Mizgog
lines.text: Give them here and I'll check that they really are MY beads, before I give you your reward. You'll like it, it's an amulet of accuracy.
